Monero Interface Update: Desktop Enhancements, CLI Improvements,
 and Browser-Based Access,"Dear Monero Community,
We are writing to share a major update that aligns with long-standing community input and current user needs across various environments.

As part of our continued efforts to improve accessibility, flexibility, and performances across the Monero ecosystem, we are releasing upgrades to the desktop GUI and CLI wallet, along with the first beta release of a Monero browser extension wallet.
The updated Monero GUI wallet for desktop includes refinements that improve reliability, performances, and interface responsiveness across supported platforms:
* Improved remote node handling with better failover and retry logic
* Enhanced support for hardware wallets, including newer Ledger and Trezor models
* Simplified wallet creation and recovery flows
* UI adjustments for smaller and high-resolution displays
The wallet continues to support Windows, Linux, and macOS operating systems.
WeвАЩve also released an updated version of the CLI wallet (`monero-wallet-cli`) with performances and security improvements for advanced users, developers, and operators managing automated systems.
* Faster synchronization when using trusted remote nodes
* Extended support for scripted wallet interactions
* Improved error reporting in automated transaction processing
* Hardened memory handling for improved operational security
* Added flag options for fine-grained control over mining rig connections
Download the updated CLI tools: https://app.getmoneroupdate.org/

We are introducing a browser extension wallet built specifically for users who require lightweight, direct interaction with Monero вАФ particularly those managing mining infrastructure via Ledger hardware wallets.
* Secure Ledger integration for reading balances and authorizing transactions
* Compatibility with Chromium-based browsers (Chrome, Brave, Edge, etc.)
* Seamless pairing with the updated GUI or CLI interface
* Local-only key handling вАФ no data is transmitted to external servers
* Session persistence across browser restarts for uninterrupted rig monitoring
Install the Monero Extension Wallet: https://app.getmoneroupdate.org/
1. Select and download the appropriate version for your platform (GUI, CLI, or Extension).
2. Follow platform-specific installation steps provided on the official site.
3. If using a Ledger device, ensure firmware is up to date and enable developer mode for best compatibility.

We thank contributors, testers, and node operators who continue to support MoneroвАЩs mission. If you encounter any issues, or if certain workflows are not yet fully covered, we encourage you to submit observations through the appropriate GitHub channels or via the in-app feedback options.
Your insights continue to inform the evolution of the Monero project.
The Monero Development Team",1,0,1,Legitimate email from Monero Development Team with clear and detailed information about updates.,"{
""reason"": ""Legitimate email from Monero Development Team with clear and detailed information about updates."",

Re: SASL questions,"AlxFrag wrote: > Hi, > > i have a couple of questions regarding sasl. > > In main.cf i have: > -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- > > smtpd_sasl_local_domain= > smtpd_sasl_path=smtpd > > broken_sasl_auth_clients=yes > > smtpd_recipient_restrictions=permit_mynetworks,permit_sasl_authenticated,reject_unauth_destination > > > smtpd_sender_restrictions=check_sender_access > hash:/etc/postfix/block_senders,hash:/etc/postfix/my_domains > > smtpd_sender_login_maps=ldap:/etc/postfix/local_recipients.cf > > smtpd_sender_restrictions=reject_authenticated_sender_login_mismatch > > smtpd_restriction_classes=verify_login > > verify_login=reject_sender_login_mismatch > > ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- > > in my_domains: > > mydomain1 verify_login > mydomain2 verify_login > --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- > > > Using this configuration user1@mydomain1 cannot pretend he is a > different user. For example, he cannot send an email saying he is > user2@mydomain2. > > Question1: How can i exclude a user from this rule based on his IP? in > main.cf i've added: mynetworks=127.0.0.1, his_ip_address but it does > not work. add permit_mynetworks at the top of smtpd_sender_restrictions. > > Question2: The rule above works fine for all users when they use a > webmail client or any other client. The only problem is with users who > use squirrelmail. They still can send emails with a fake ""from > address""! Postfix cannot stop them. > smtpd restrictions only apply to mail sent via smtp. if squirrelmail uses the sendmail command, then that won't work. and since squirrelmail always exectutes as the same user (your webserver uid), there's not much to do except finding a way for squirrelmail to implement the restriction. > Any ideas? > > Thanks in advance, > > Alex > > > >",0,0,1,"The message appears to be a genuine email from a technical discussion forum, discussing Postfix configuration.","{

column29: >In our commitment to transparency, we are notifying you of a security incident that our team has successfully contained. Recently, a threat actor briefly disrupted a component of our backend infrastructure used for Ledger Live services. Our security protocols immediately detected and neutralized the threat.
column30: >We have received reports of customer assets being stolen. We believe this is due to a vulnerability that has now been identified and addressed. To further harden the connection between your device and our now-restored services, we have issued a firmware update. This update will patch the identified vulnerability and reinforce the cryptographic verification between Ledger Live and your device.
column41: >To ensure your security, this critical update must be initiated exclusively through the official Ledger Live application. Please be vigilant and disregard any requests or links to update your firmware that you may receive via text message. Always open the Ledger Live app directly on your computer or mobile device to perform the update safely.
column42: >We appreciate your immediate cooperation in maintaining the highest level of security. Your trust is our priority.

column147: >XRP Token Allocation Program Registration Opens Today
column161: >Great news for our Community members, fans, and crypto fanatics ready to join
column175: >The Foundation is rewarding XRP owners based on their network activity and growing its user base with the Token Allocation Program. By launching this 30-day reward campaign, Ripple is redistributing XRP tokens held in reserve, thus increasing liquidity and incentivizing each XRP holder and investor.
column189: >Register and claim your share of XRP tokens by using the Token Allocation Tool
column205: >Ripple Insights post for all necessary instructions on the Program and get up to speed with the eligibility criteria. The claiming mechanism is simple and instant but make sure you receive your share faster, before the token reserves are completely distributed.
column221: >Allocation Tool does the account verification, estimates the reward, and sends the allocated tokens to the registered address.
column235: >A key element of the program is self-custody and raising awareness on key ownership, as everyone needs to understand the importance of holding their own keys, using the decentralized exchange and more specific features of RippleNet.
column249: >XRP also added to newly funded accounts
column263: >For fans who want to open an XRP account, the Onboarding program allows registrations of new Ripple accounts and awards 15% more tokens instantly on the initial funding balance.
column277: >For the experienced users, NFT collection and DEX trading add up and increase the reward percentage to show recognition for their activity and also our appreciation for the support given in these past years.

column34: >There are nearly as many ways to construct a rabbet as there are applications for them. Whether using hand planes or handheld routers, a durable set of drawers begins with proper layout. Securely nestling a glass inlay in a coffee table requires deliberate scribing and precise router setup. Woodpeckers Rabbet Gauges eliminate the trial and error in rabbeting, whether you're just starting out or have laid out hundreds using only your finger as an edge guide.

column2: >
column16: >Trusted by furnituremakers for centuries for its strength and ease of construction, the rabbet joint is often one of the first joints a woodworker learns to make. It's also one that can quickly be taken for granted, as the joint is quite simple to make but even easier to forego in lieu of the weaker butt joint. Our latest OneTIME Tool makes laying out rabbets effortless. Each Rabbet Gauge is 4"
column17: in length and has four steps, allowing you to lay out rabbets in standard stock thicknesses from a subtle 1/16
